node_pool(3) Library Functions Manual node_pool(3)

NAME

node_pool - Memory pool for packet nodes to reduce malloc/free overhead.

SYNOPSIS

#include <include/ascii-chat/network/packet/queue.h>

Data Fields


atomic_ptr_t free_list
Stack of free nodes (LIFO for cache locality) - atomic for lock-free access. packet_node_t * nodes
Pre-allocated array of all nodes. size_t pool_size
Total number of nodes in pool. atomic_t used_count
Number of nodes currently in use - atomic for lock-free tracking.

Detailed Description

Memory pool for packet nodes to reduce malloc/free overhead.

Pre-allocates a fixed-size array of packet nodes and maintains a lock-free free list using atomic compare-and-swap operations. This eliminates per-packet malloc/free calls and avoids lock contention for high-frequency packet queue operations.

Note

Pool operations are thread-safe via lock-free atomics (no mutexes).

If pool is exhausted, operations fall back to malloc/free.

Definition at line 167 of file queue.h.
